tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

content.text: {"number":58299,"title":"[ResourceGraph] Docs: Initial README gaps in Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph 1.0.0-beta.1","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph` **Service directory:** `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/` **Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (post-release version bump for PR #58257) by `@azure-sdk` ## What Changed Initial public release (1.0.0-beta.1) of `Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph`, which enables declarative provisioning of Azure Resource Graph shared queries using the Azure Provisioning library pattern. The package exposes the `ResourceGraphQuery` resource type backed by `Microsoft.ResourceGraph/queries@2024-04-01`. ## Gaps Found - **Empty "Authenticate the Client" section**: The `### Authenticate the Client` sub-heading under "Getting started" exists but contains zero content. Consumers need guidance on what credentials to supply when using `azd` or the Azure Provisioning infrastructure APIs. - **Empty "Next steps" section**: The `## Next steps` heading exists but has no content — no links to API reference, related provisioning packages (e.g., `Azure.Provisioning.Resources`), or the `azd` deployment workflow. - **`FromExisting` pattern is undocumented**: `ResourceGraphQuery.FromExisting(...)` allows referencing a pre-existing Resource Graph query in a larger infrastructure definition, which is a key Provisioning library pattern. This method is not mentioned anywhere in the README. - **Output properties not explained**: The read-only output properties `ResultKind`, `ModifiedOn`, and `Id` can be referenced in downstream Bicep expressions. The README has no mention of reading outputs or using them in multi-resource configurations. 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/README.md` — **edit** 1. Fill in the empty `### Authenticate the Client` section (after the `### Prerequisites` section): ```markdown ### Authenticate the Client `Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph` is a declarative provisioning library — it generates Bicep/ARM templates rather than calling Azure APIs directly at runtime. Authentication is handled by the deployment tooling: - When using **Azure Developer CLI (`azd`)**, run `azd auth login` to authenticate before deploying. - When deploying programmatically, provide an `ArmClient` with your preferred [`TokenCredential`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/README.md) (e.g., `DefaultAzureCredential`). For more information on authentication, see the [Azure Identity README](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/README.md). ``` 2. Add a second example under `## Examples` for the `FromExisting` pattern (after the existing query-creation example): ```markdown ### Reference an Existing Resource Graph Query Use `FromExisting` to reference a shared query that is already deployed, so you can wire its outputs into other resources: ``` ResourceGraphQuery existingQuery = ResourceGraphQuery.FromExisting(nameof(existingQuery)); existingQuery.Name = "my-existing-query"; infra.Add(existingQuery); ``` ``` 3. Fill in the `## Next steps` section: ```markdown ## Next steps - [Azure Resource Graph documentation]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) - [API reference for Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) - [Azure Provisioning library overview](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning/README.md) - [Deploy infrastructure with Azure Developer CLI (`azd`)]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) - [Azure.Provisioning.Resources — use ResourceGroup, Subscription, and other ARM primitives alongside your query](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning.Resources/README.md) ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets **Snippet `ResourceGraphQueryFromExisting`** Write the sample code in a new test file at: `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/tests/Samples/SampleFromExisting.cs` ```csharp // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved. // Licensed under the MIT License. using Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph; using NUnit.Framework; namespace Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph.Tests.Samples; public class SampleFromExisting { [Test] public void FromExistingExample() { #region Snippet:ResourceGraphQueryFromExisting Infrastructure infra = new(); ResourceGraphQuery existingQuery = ResourceGraphQuery.FromExisting(nameof(existingQuery)); existingQuery.Name = "my-existing-query"; infra.Add(existingQuery); #endregion } } ``` Add the following snippet placeholder in `README.md` under the new "Reference an Existing Resource Graph Query" sub-section (replace the manually-pasted code block shown in Step 1 above): ````markdown ```C# Snippet:ResourceGraphQueryFromExisting ``` ```` The `Update-Snippets.ps1` script will fill in the code automatically. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/README.md` is currently missing content in these sections: | Section | Status | Action | |---------|--------|--------| | Getting started > Authenticate the Client | ❌ Empty | Add content per Step 1 | | Key concepts | ✅ Present (brief, acceptable for initial beta) | No change needed | | Examples | ⚠️ One example only | Add `FromExisting` example per Step 1 | | Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | No change needed | | Next steps | ❌ Empty | Add links per Step 1 | | Contributing | ✅ Present | No change needed | Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next: 1. `dotnet build sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 resourcegraph` — confirms README snippet placeholders are filled with code from test files; check `git diff` after running 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 resourcegraph` — regenerates public API listing; no new public types expected for this docs-only change 5. `dotnet format sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24575559392/agentic_workflow) · ● 567.7K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58299","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-17T16:32:44Z","updated_at":"2026-04-17T16:32:44Z"}

{"total_count":1,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":3292095671,"number":51705,"state":"closed","state_reason":"completed","locked":true,"title":"CosmosDBAccountCapability to implement IEquatable<CosmosDBAccountCapability>","body":"## Background
This is a "sub-issue" to one raised in the Aspire repo: [Upgrading to 9.4 causes duplicate capabilities in Cosmos DB and fails deployment](https://github.com/dotnet/aspire/issues/10808).

## Request
It would be great if `CosmosDBAccountCapability` could implement `IEquatable<CosmosDBAccountCapability>`. It would make the "upserting" of CosmosDB capabilities within the Aspire framework much easier if it were simple to compare 2 `CosmosDBAccountCapability` for equality based on their `Name` property alone.","author_association":"NONE","user":{"login":"goodcoffeecode","id":1698190,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE2OTgxOTA=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1698190?v=4","html_url":"https://github.com/goodcoffeecode","gravatar_id":"","type":"User","site_admin":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/users/goodcoffeecode","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/goodcoffeecode/events{/privacy}","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/goodcoffeecode/following{/other_user}","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/goodcoffeecode/followers","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/goodcoffeecode/gists{/gist_id}","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/goodcoffeecode/orgs","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/goodcoffeecode/received_events","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/goodcoffeecode/repos","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/goodcoffeecode/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/goodcoffeecode/subscriptions"},"labels":[{"id":1178448111,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/Mgmt","name":"Mgmt","color":"ffeb77","description":"This issue is related to a management package.","default":false,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxMTc4NDQ4MTEx"},{"id":1353316183,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/customer-reported","name":"customer-reported","color":"3800e0","description":"Issues that are reported by GitHub users external to the Azure organization.","default":false,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxMzUzMzE2MTgz"},{"id":1369687945,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/question","name":"question","color":"eaa875","description":"The issue doesn't require a change to the product in order to be resolved. arguments.message: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

arguments.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue identifies documentation gaps in the initial `Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph` 1.0.0-beta.1 README, including empty "Authenticate the Client" and "Next steps" sections, an undocumented `FromExisting` pattern, and missing output property documentation.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph` 1.0.0-beta.1
**Affected API:** `ResourceGraphQuery`, `ResourceGraphQuery.FromExisting(...)`
**Scenarios:**
- Empty `### Authenticate the Client` section with no guidance on credential setup
- Empty `## Next steps` section with no links to API reference or related packages
- `ResourceGraphQuery.FromExisting(...)` pattern not documented in README
- Read-only output properties (`ResultKind`, `ModifiedOn`, `Id`) not explained for use in downstream Bicep expressions

**Root ask:** Fill README gaps with authentication guidance, a `FromExisting` example, output property documentation, and populated "Next steps" links.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is an auto-generated documentation gap issue triggered by post-release version bump commit `[REDACTED]` for PR #58257.

The issue includes a detailed implementation guide:
- Modify `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/README.md`
- Add a new test sample file at `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/tests/Samples/SampleFromExisting.cs`
- Run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 resourcegraph` to wire snippet placeholders

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review the current README at `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/README.md` to confirm empty sections
2. Implement content per the implementation guide in the issue body
3. Run `dotnet build` and `dotnet test --filter TestCategory!=Live` in the resourcegraph directory, then `Update-Snippets.ps1` and `Export-API.ps1` to validate
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Provisioning` — package name `Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph` unambiguously starts with `Azure.Provisioning`, the standard prefix for the Provisioning library family
- **Service:** `Resource Graph` — the package wraps the `Microsoft.ResourceGraph/queries` resource type, mapping directly to the Azure Resource Graph service
- **Confidence:** High — both labels are clearly implied by the package name and issue content; no ambiguity between competing services or categories
</details>

<details>
<summary>👥 Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %Resource Graph` (line 851) — requires only "Resource Graph"; the predicted labels include "Resource Graph" → match
- **AzureSdkOwners:** none listed
- **ServiceOwners:** @chiragg4u
- **Routing action:** Added "Service Attention" label; issue left unassigned (ServiceOwners path, no AzureSdkOwners); no "needs-team-attention" added (not customer-reported — bot-filed issue)
- **Scan notes:** Entries from line 1228 down to 852 all require `%Mgmt` or `%CodeGen` labels which this issue does not have → skipped. First match encountered (bottom-to-top) was `%Resource Graph` at line 851.
</details>
